text.title: csharp-dotnet-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for csharp-dotnet-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Anemic domain model: entities are data containers; logic lives in services
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Logic scatters across services, making invariants hard to reason about. Multiple services may mutate the same entity inconsistently. Domain rules leak into application and presentation layers.

failure-mode.description: Exposing domain entities directly in API responses
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: API becomes tightly coupled to the domain schema. Changing the domain (adding a field, renaming a property) breaks all clients. It also exposes internal state that shouldn't be visible.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Long-running operations block the request thread. Use async/await and consider background jobs for heavy work.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Invalid data reaches the domain, requiring defensive coding everywhere. Validators in handlers catch invalid requests early and return 400 Bad Request.

failure-mode.description: MediatR and AutoMapper hallucinations: agents emit code against old API versions
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents use APIs from MediatR v9, AutoMapper v10. Both are now commercial. Pin versions in the prompt and reject IRequestPostProcessor. Better: specify which library (MediatR vs Mediator vs Wolverine)—they have incompatible APIs.

failure-mode.description: Anemic-domain regression when asked to add behavior
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents produce UserService.Promote(user) instead of user.Promote(). Constrain with structured output: where_does_this_live: aggregate|service|handler and reject service for state-transition logic.

failure-mode.description: Public setters return despite CQRS patterns
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Code-gen agents emit `public string Name { get; set; }` because that's the training-data norm. Force `private set;` via prompt example; lint catches the rest.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents inline auth checks, mapping, and calls into controller actions. Always require: action signature → mediator.Send → return. Nothing else.

failure-mode.description: EF Core entities leak into API responses
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents return entities to skip the DTO step. Always pair the controller change with Dto, AutoMapper Profile, and Query handler; reject PRs without them.
